title: Add Terraform scaffold and state backend
description: Create the Terraform infra/ folder structure, module skeletons, dev and prod environments, and a bootstrap script to create the S3 state bucket and DynamoDB lock table. Does not touch template.yaml or any existing application files.

acceptance_criteria.given: the infra/ folder does not exist
acceptance_criteria.then: infra/modules/ contains dynamodb/, lambda/, api-gateway/, s3-cloudfront/, ssm/, vpc/ directories each with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf
dev/ and prod/ both exist each containing main.tf, variables.tf, terraform.tfvars, backend.tf
it prints the aws commands it would run without executing them

non_functional_requirements.security: No secrets or account IDs hardcoded in any .tf file. State bucket must have versioning and encryption enabled.
non_functional_requirements.compliance: All resources must follow naming convention: {project}-{environment}-{resource}

definition_of_done: infra/ folder structure created with all modules and environments
terraform init succeeds in dev environment
agent_instructions: Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md before writing any .tf files. If the constitution file does not exist, stop and write an escalation — do not proceed without it. Do not modify template.yaml or any files outside infra/, scripts/, .github/, and .gitignore.
